rationale: Ground truth contains no substantive concerns (just an 'lgtm' approval), so both candidates correctly reach APPROVE and recall is moot for both. X reads well but its specific citations don't hold up: its cited test-file line numbers (2638/2651/2720) don't match the diff's hunk positions (2530/2543/2612), and it invents unverifiable specifics (a precise upstream PR number 'vllm#46022' and a 'concrete repro' from the PR body) that aren't grounded in the given diff. Y's line citations (2533/2546/2615, 1017) align exactly with the diff hunk offsets, and it flags a legitimate pre-existing edge case (None handling in diffusion mode) without overreaching into unverifiable claims, making it more trustworthy and better grounded.
